subtopicMappings.description: No canonical node for describing distribution shape, symmetry, unimodal/bimodal or skewness.
No canonical node for the idea that sample diagrams become more representative of theoretical distributions as sample size increases.
No canonical node for identifying distinct sub-populations or outliers in scatter diagrams.
No canonical node for identifying outliers using the 2-sd or 1.5*IQR rules.
No canonical node for data cleaning, missing data or errors.
No canonical node for expected frequency calculated from probability.
No canonical node for expected frequencies associated with a binomial distribution.
No canonical node for SI fundamental quantities and units length, time and mass.
